description: Illustrative, static stress scenarios for local research triage only. These are not live data, forecasts, recommendations, or trading instructions.

scenarios.name: AI capex digestion
scenarios.description: A hypothetical moderation in AI infrastructure spending forces review of demand, utilization, and margin assumptions.

shocks.name: Cloud capex pause
shocks.description: Large cloud buyers slow incremental AI infrastructure commitments while absorbing prior capacity.

shocks.name: Accelerator lead times normalize
shocks.description: Supply availability improves faster than demand visibility, changing the scarcity narrative.

scenarios.name: Digital ads policy tightening
scenarios.description: A hypothetical policy review increases scrutiny of targeting, reporting, and data-use practices.

shocks.name: Ad targeting limits
shocks.description: New restrictions reduce flexibility in targeted digital advertising products.

shocks.name: Platform reporting costs
shocks.description: Compliance work absorbs management attention and raises operating complexity.

scenarios.name: Premium device demand fade
scenarios.description: A hypothetical consumer replacement-cycle slowdown tests hardware resilience and services offset claims.

shocks.name: Replacement cycle extends
shocks.description: Consumers extend premium device replacement cycles despite stable installed-base engagement.

shocks.name: Launch cadence slip
shocks.description: Component timing creates narrower launch windows for high-end devices.

scenarios.name: Cloud margin compression
scenarios.description: A hypothetical cost absorption issue tests whether AI infrastructure utilization can support margins.

shocks.name: Utilization lag
shocks.description: New AI infrastructure capacity takes longer to reach efficient utilization.

shocks.name: Enterprise budget delay
shocks.description: Enterprise buyers defer some new AI platform commitments into later planning cycles.
